title: 1.1 Reasoning chain (Why → Who → What → How)
body:
- **Why (SMART goal).** Double the site's organic traffic: go from **80,000 to 160,000 monthly visits in 3 months**, making the site far more findable on Google through an SEO strategy applied to a **new hub-and-pagination structure**. Example lever: a **"perder peso" (weight loss) hub** captures one of the highest-volume searches in Mexico and, on its own, has the potential to double traffic.
 - **Secondary goal:** double (**2x**) the number of **qualified leads** that reach the Asesor.
 - **Tertiary goal:** reduce **customer response time** through the **voice agent** (immediate 24/7 service).
- **Who (actors).**
 - *Primary:* the two CORE archetypes of the consumer journey (§2): **Family Wellbeing Manager** (Family CWO, Priority 1, LTV 3x–4x) and **Urban Hybrid Executive** (Third Spacer, Priority 2). They arrive via local Google search and land through one of 4 doors (home, club, amenity/class, goal page).
 - *Secondary:* the **Asesor** (sales) who receives the brief and schedules the guided visit; the **voice agent** that answers and coordinates.
 - *Offstage:* trainers who define exercises in the first session; the marketing/SEO team that governs the hubs.
- **What (measurable behavior / Jobs to be Done).** The visitor must: **find** the site on Google → **complete** the Experiencia Ideal questionnaire → **leave** their contact information → **schedule** the guided visit. Measured by: organic traffic, questionnaire completion rate, qualified leads, and time to first response.
- **How (tactics/UI).** An **SEO hub** architecture (high-volume indexable pages) feeding the **Experiencia Ideal** flow: an **adaptive** guided questionnaire (15–21 questions depending on gender, pause, children, and weight path) that delivers a personalized recommendation (Block 1 weights · Block 2 cardio · Block 3 classes) + contact capture + brief for the Asesor + voice agent.

title: 2. Personas and Customer Journey
body: > **Authoritative source:** `Consumer Journey — Sports World` (client annex document; deep secondary research: reputation + user behavior in premium fitness MX). Benchmarks: Sports World NPS 47.3 (Q3 2025, year high) · Smart Fit receives 8× visits for every 1 to SW (PREDIK) · sector median annual retention 66.4% (HFA 2025) · 50% of the sector's new members cancel within the first 6 months (IHRSA).

title: 2.1 The two primary CORE archetypes (one backbone, tactical divergences)
body:
The journey has the **same backbone for both** — 8 identical phases, same touchpoints, same adaptive questionnaire. The divergences are **tactical** (what to emphasize per phase, which amenities carry weight, which pains dominate, what tone to use), not structural.

**P1 — Family Wellbeing Manager ("Family CWO") · CORE Priority 1 · owner of the LTV (3x–4x the individual membership).**
35–50 years old · NSE AB/C+ · married · 1–2 children aged 4–12 · high-density areas (Del Valle, Polanco, Satélite, Interlomas, Pedregal). *"Vivo manejando a los niños. Si pago un club, que me devuelva tiempo, no que me quite más."*
- **JTBD:** the afternoon disappears into the carpool (3–4 fragmented hours); if they pay for a club, they want to **get time back** — train while the kids are safe and learning at FitKidz, without waiting in the car or coordinating three locations. The club is the **operations center of the family afternoon**.
- **Moat fit: MAXIMUM** — FitKidz solves the after-school logistics; the pool is essential for the children's development; high switching cost (switching = reorganizing the family afternoon).
- **Behavior:** local search focused on the children ("gym con alberca para niños en [zona]", "natación niños cerca de [escuela]") from the school/home geography. Tolerates reputational friction, but **any child-safety incident is an absolute deal-breaker**. **Slow** decision: **1–3 weeks** (consults their partner and other parents); checks "[club] kids" reviews.

**P2 — Urban Hybrid Executive ("Third Spacer") · CORE Priority 2 · justifies the premium price.**
28–45 years old · independent professional or corporate employee on a hybrid/remote scheme · lives or works in an area with a legacy club (Antara, Reforma, Polanco, Santa Fe, Interlomas). *"Si arranco el día sin moverme, llego plano a todo lo demás."*
- **JTBD:** needs a **third space** between home (too small for a full workday) and the office — a place to break up the day, train, shower in premium conditions, and handle the work→rest-of-life transition. The club is **infrastructure for their hybrid day**.
- **Moat fit: HIGH** — extended amenities (steam room, sauna, towels, internet, lobby/coworking) absent in low-cost gyms and boutiques; multiclub (club near home or near the office depending on the day). Strategy: monetize time-on-site.
- **Behavior:** hyperlocal or amenity-driven search ("gym con vapor en Polanco", "club con coworking"); decides faster than the Family CWO; reads 5–15 reviews of the specific club before deciding; demands fast confirmation (WhatsApp).

**Secondary:** P3 Asesor (receives the brief with flags; converts the visit without re-asking questions) · P4 Voice agent/BES (serves 24/7, schedules, transfers to a human).

title: 2.2 The journey — 8 phases × 6 layers
body:
| Phase | What happens | Tactical divergence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **F1 Trigger** | Recognizes a physical cost (back pain, exhaustion, medical warning) and decides to change | CWO: post-school logistics chaos · Exec: home-office isolation, third space |
| **F2 Initial consideration** | Searches Google with local intent; lands through one of **4 doors** (home, specific club, amenity/class, goal page) and receives the **non-blocking** invitation to the questionnaire | CWO: school/home geography · Exec: office geography ("50% of SW visitors live in the area; 19% work in it" — PREDIK) |
| **F3 Active evaluation** | With the questionnaire: sees clubs/amenities/classes **filtered to their profile**; reads Google reviews of the suggested club; compares price vs Smart Fit ("93% say reviews influence them") | CWO: filters for child safety/FitKidz/pool/cleanliness · Exec: peak-hour crowding, instructors, locker rooms, internet |
| **F4 Tour request** | Books via the site (Path B), BES (Path A), or a call; appointment confirmed **in real time via API** with the client's booking system | CWO: 4–7 PM window, asks to bring a child · Exec: early or post-work, WhatsApp confirmation |
| **F5 Conversion** | Tour, membership options, decision; signs the same day or returns within 1–7 days | CWO consults their partner; Exec decides faster |
| **F6 Onboarding (1–90d)** | First class, first booking in the app, genuine welcome | The "person who works out" identity is fragile; the app has documented stability issues when booking |
| **F7 Habit (3–12m)** | Recurring bookings, pauses, variety | **Silent plateau** (weeks 4–6): the sector's highest churn |
| **F8 Loyalty loop** | Renews, refers, expands (family, multiclub, upgrade) | CWO: family expansion · Exec: multiclub/PT |

**Emotional curve:** decision peak at F4–F5 → **churn valley at F6–F7** (silent plateau) → consolidated loyalty at F8.

```mermaid
journey
 title Consumer Journey — 8 phases (emotional curve)
 section Decide
 F1 Trigger: 1: CWO, Exec
 F2 Consideration (4 doors + questionnaire): 3: CWO, Exec
 F3 Evaluation (reviews, comparison): 3: CWO, Exec
 section Decision peak
 F4 Tour (real-time API): 5: CWO, Exec
 F5 Conversion (signing): 5: CWO, Exec
 section Churn valley
 F6 Onboarding (1st class, app): 2: CWO, Exec
 F7 Habit (plateau weeks 4-6): 1: CWO, Exec
 section Loyalty
 F8 Loyalty loop (renews, refers, expands): 5: CWO, Exec
```

title: 2.3 Moments of Truth — where staying or leaving is decided
body:
| MoT | Phase | Moment | Risk / intervention |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 01 | F2 | **The questionnaire on the landing page** — the system's first intervention. **Non-blocking** pop-up, only **once per active session**; if dismissed, it remains visible as a discreet button | Invite without interrupting; each entry door reveals intent (pre-fill per Rule 20) |
| 02 | F3 | **The review check of the specific club** — reads 5–15 Google reviews of the suggested club | Polarized reviews = paralysis; recent mentions of maintenance issues/apathetic staff = they go off to investigate another club |
| 03 | F4–F5 | **First contact from the Asesor** + consistency across 49 clubs (sector show rate 60–75%, close 30–45%) | Depends on: how prepared the Asesor arrives (→ brief, Appendix G), it not feeling like a sales pitch, and confirmation speed (solved by the real-time API) |
| 04 | F6 | **First class and first booking in the app** | Fragile identity: an intimidating class, an instructor who doesn't notice the newcomer, an app that fails when booking, or a cold front desk → the membership wobbles from day 1 |
| 05 | F7 | **The silent plateau (weeks 4–6)** — the sector's highest-churn moment | Intervene proactively: explain that the plateau is NORMAL, recalibrate the program, free session with a PT — or churn begins that very day |
| 06 | F7 | **The return after the first work crunch** (absence of 1–2+ weeks) | If no one notices the absence or eases the return, the silent consideration of canceling begins |

title: 2.4 Journey → site architecture mapping
body:
| Phase | Informational need | Section/page | CTA |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| F1 | Know that an accessible premium option exists | (upstream branding — outside the site's scope) | — |
| F2 | Confirm club type, locations, price range | Home · GMB profiles (49) · Club detail (local SEO entry) | "Encuentra tu club" locator |
| F3 | Validate the specific club: real photos, schedules, classes, reviews, amenities | Club detail (49) · Class detail · Membership · Help Center | "Agenda tu visita" (primary) · "Diseña tu experiencia" (secondary) |
| F4 | Confirm the appointment, reminders, arrive prepared | Path A (BES) · Path B (direct booking) · WhatsApp templates | Tour confirmation |
| F5 | Sign with clear expectations for the first 30 days | (in club, mostly off-site) + Help Center «Tu primera visita» | — |
| F6 | Book the 1st class, understand the app, genuine welcome | App · Help Center «Tu primera visita» · member portal | Class booking |
| F7 | Recurring bookings, pauses, variety | App · Class detail · Personal Training · Help Center «Soy socio» | Booking · Freeze · PT |
| F8 | Renew, refer, expand | Member portal · FitKidz hub · Memberships | Upgrade / referrals |

> **Precedence note:** the journey document describes a "10 questions, 1 minute" instrument. The **official agreed questionnaire (15 base + 6 conditional, Rule 18)** prevails; from the journey we adopt the adaptive logic per entry door, the non-blocking pop-up (1×/session, persistent as a button), and the Moments of Truth. The time-to-complete goal must be measured against the official instrument (abandonment risk already documented in §10.3).

---
